# Rosalind Problem: Computing GC Content in Ada

## Problem Description
Given at most 10 DNA strings in FASTA format (of length at most 1 kbp each), return the ID of the string having the highest GC content, followed by the GC content of that string. The GC content is the percentage of nucleotides that are either G or C.

## Solution

```ada
with Ada.Text_IO;           use Ada.Text_IO;
with Ada.Strings.Unbounded; use Ada.Strings.Unbounded;
with Ada.Float_Text_IO;     use Ada.Float_Text_IO;

procedure Computing_GC_Content is
   
   type DNA_String is array (Positive range <>) of Character;
   
   -- Function to calculate GC content percentage
   function GC_Content(DNA : String) return Float is
      G_Count, C_Count, Total_Length : Natural := 0;
   begin
      Total_Length := DNA'Length;
      
      for I in DNA'Range loop
         case DNA(I) is
            when 'G' | 'g' =>
               G_Count := G_Count + 1;
            when 'C' | 'c' =>
               C_Count := C_Count + 1;
            when others =>
               null;
         end case;
      end loop;
      
      if Total_Length = 0 then
         return 0.0;
      else
         return Float(G_Count + C_Count) * 100.0 / Float(Total_Length);
      end if;
   end GC_Content;
   
   -- Function to read FASTA format
   function Read_FASTA_String return Unbounded_String is
      Line : Unbounded_String;
      Result : Unbounded_String := Null_Unbounded_String;
   begin
      while not End_Of_File loop
         Get_Line(Line);
         if Length(Line) > 0 and then Element(Line, 1) /= '>' then
            Result := Result & Line;
         elsif Length(Line) > 0 and then Element(Line, 1) = '>' then
            exit;
         end if;
      end loop;
      return Result;
   end Read_FASTA_String;
   
   -- Main processing
   Max_GC : Float := 0.0;
   Max_ID : Unbounded_String := Null_Unbounded_String;
   Current_ID : Unbounded_String := Null_Unbounded_String;
   Current_DNA : Unbounded_String := Null_Unbounded_String;
   Line : Unbounded_String;
   
begin
   -- Process each FASTA entry
   while not End_Of_File loop
      Get_Line(Line);
      
      if Length(Line) > 0 and then Element(Line, 1) = '>' then
         -- Process previous sequence if exists
         if Length(Current_DNA) > 0 then
            declare
               GC_Percent : Float := GC_Content(To_String(Current_DNA));
            begin
               if GC_Percent > Max_GC then
                  Max_GC := GC_Percent;
                  Max_ID := Current_ID;
               end if;
            end;
         end if;
         
         -- Set new ID
         Current_ID := Line;
         Current_DNA := Null_Unbounded_String;
      else
         -- Append DNA sequence
         Current_DNA := Current_DNA & Line;
      end if;
   end loop;
   
   -- Process last sequence
   if Length(Current_DNA) > 0 then
      declare
         GC_Percent : Float := GC_Content(To_String(Current_DNA));
      begin
         if GC_Percent > Max_GC then
            Max_GC := GC_Percent;
            Max_ID := Current_ID;
         end if;
      end;
   end if;
   
   -- Output results
   Put_Line(To_String(Max_ID));
   Put(Max_GC, Fore => 1, Aft => 6, Exp => 0);
   New_Line;
   
end Computing_GC_Content;
```

## Explanation

This Ada solution:

1. **GC_Content Function**: Calculates the percentage of GC nucleotides in a DNA string by counting G and C characters and dividing by total length.

2. **Main Algorithm**: 
   - Reads FASTA format input line by line
   - Identifies sequence IDs (lines starting with '>')
   - Accumulates DNA sequences until next ID is found
   - Calculates GC content for each sequence
   - Keeps track of the sequence with maximum GC content

3. **Output Format**: 
   - Prints the ID of the sequence with highest GC content
   - Prints the GC percentage with 6 decimal places

## Sample Input/Output

**Input:**
```
>Rosalind_6404
CCTGCGGAAGATCGGCACTAGAATAGCCAGAACCGTTTCTCTGACGTCCAGCGTAATCTTCTT
>Rosalind_5959
CCATCGGTAGCGCATCCTTAGTCCAATTAAGTCCCTATCCAGGCGCTCCGCCGAAGGTCT
>Rosalind_0808
